nodes.body: This looks like it should be a Singleton
This looks like it should be a Singleton
if our OpenAPI generator could support different success response codes, this is a spot where 201 vs 200 could mean something different
hmm, is this the normal pattern for other api implementation classes?  it seems to me like the Actor System should be shared across resources

I believe this is the normal pattern for using the generated HTTP clients. See  [DatasetRwFiletaskManager](https://github.com/cerebrotech/domino/blob/02c085d6f9d9411af0e4514397d4268cf8d6fa6c/dataset-rw/dataset-rw-impl/src/main/scala/domino/datasetrw/impl/DatasetRwFiletaskManager.scala#L24) and [MLFlowClientImpl](https://github.com/cerebrotech/domino/blob/02c085d6f9d9411af0e4514397d4268cf8d6fa6c/mlflow-client/implementation/src/main/scala/domino/mlflowclient/implementation/MlflowClientImpl.scala#L28). Do you think this is something we want to change, or is it okay to adhere?
cc @fernandoacorreia is this something we want to be doing?  Creating a new ActorSystem?
Fixed so we inject the ActorSystem instead of declaring a new one inside of the class
@ddl-tnguyen as discussed, let's inject this as a class parameter, and on the Guice provider let it pick the default ActorSystem

I'm not sure I followed this correctly -- do we already have an "internal" model monitoring API in nucleus that DMM calls?
If so, does this function need to part of a public API, or could it be internal only for callbacks from model serving?

Yeah I agree it's a bit confusing. The "internal" API (implemented at [ModelMonitoringApiImpl](https://github.com/cerebrotech/domino/blob/02c085d6f9d9411af0e4514397d4268cf8d6fa6c/model-monitoring/implementation/src/main/scala/domino/modelmonitoring/implementation/ModelMonitoringApiImpl.scala#L18)) is actually for nucleus to make calls to DMM Plier. If you look at the implementation, we're actually just making HTTP calls to plier. The yamls for that API live in `dmm-api-backend` and we just use the `domino-play-server` template as a way of bringing those types into nucleus as scala code. 

We don't expose that API anywhere and it can't be hit by an external service, so that's why I created the separate public API.
Do we need this dependency on server?
Yes unfortunately the GlobalPermissions we check in `ModelMonitoringPublicApiImpl` from `domino.authz.global` live in server
